question: What are the main features of the NuScale multi-unit power plant?

nuscale_ch01_0835.title: SRP 17.5, Rev 1: Quality Assurance Program Description - Design Certification, Early Site Permit and New COL applicantsII.J Inspection
nuscale_ch01_0835.summary: This section summarizes NuScale's conformance with SRP 17.5, Rev 1 quality assurance criteria for design certification, early site permit, and new COL applicants. It notes that while most criteria are fully met, some areas—such as document control, inspection, and certain operational provisions—are only partially addressed, as these are the responsibility of the COL applicant referencing the certified design. Activities like identification and control of materials and special processes are also designated as the COL applicant's responsibility.

nuscale_ch01_0864.title: Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
nuscale_ch01_0864.summary: This section states that applicants referencing the NuScale Power Plant design must assess potential hazards from construction activities on multi-unit sites, ensuring the safety of both existing and new operating units as required by regulations. The evaluation must identify necessary management and administrative controls to mitigate risks and confirm that operational safety limits are not exceeded. This requirement does not apply to construction activities at a single NuScale plant with operating modules.

nuscale_ch01_0865.title: Nuclear Power Plants to be Operated on Multi-Unit Sites
nuscale_ch01_0865.summary: This section states that applicants referencing the NuScale Power Plant design must assess potential hazards from construction activities on multi-unit sites, ensuring the safety of both existing and new units as required by regulations. The evaluation must identify necessary management and administrative controls to mitigate risks and confirm that operational safety limits are not exceeded. This requirement does not apply to construction activities at a single NuScale plant with operating modules.

nuscale_ch01_0284.title: SRP BTP 8-5, Rev 3: Supplemental Guidance for Bypass and Inoperable Status Indication for Engineered Safety Features SystemsAll (B.1 thru B.6)Design Criteria Reflecting Importance of Providing Accurate Information to the Operator and Reducing the Possibility of Adversely Affecting Monitored Safety Systems
nuscale_ch01_0284.summary: This section explains that SRP BTP 8-5, Rev 3, which provides guidance on bypass and inoperable status indication for engineered safety features systems, does not apply to the NuScale electric power systems. The reason is that these systems are not classified as engineered safety features and are not relied upon to support such features in the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors engineering (HFE) considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0021.title: Engineered Safety Features and Emergency Systems
nuscale_ch01_0021.summary: The NuScale reactor maintains a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV) to reduce corrosion, facilitate leak detection, minimize heat transfer, and limit oxygen for safety. In emergencies, passive systems rapidly reduce pressure and temperature without external power, and the design eliminates large-break loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA) scenarios, resulting in improved safety and a lower core damage frequency compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0120.title: 1.8.2 Departures
nuscale_ch01_0120.summary: Section 1.8.2 describes the requirement for a Combined License (COL) applicant referencing the NuScale Power Plant design certification to provide a list of any departures from the certified design. This ensures that any differences between the certified design and the site-specific implementation are clearly identified and reviewed as part of the licensing process.

nuscale_ch01_0231.title: DSRS 7.2.10, Rev 0: Interaction Between Sense and Command Features and Other SystemsAll Varies
nuscale_ch01_0231.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to instrumentation and control systems, including setpoint methodology, auxiliary features, access control, and system interactions. For DSRS 7.2.10, it notes that while there are no specific acceptance criteria, the provided guidance is used to assess the acceptability of information regarding interactions between sense and command features and other systems. Overall, the document affirms compliance or partial compliance with relevant standards and regulatory guidance.

nuscale_ch01_0775.title: SRP 15.6.4, Rev 2: Radiological Consequences of Main Steam Line Failure Outside Containment (BWR)All - Not Applicable
nuscale_ch01_0775.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological consequences of various accident scenarios. It notes that certain criteria, such as those for main steam line failure outside containment, are not applicable to NuScale’s design, while other requirements (e.g., for coolant iodine activity and LOCA radiological consequences) are either partially conformed to or superseded by more current regulatory guidance in SRP Section 15.0.3. The NuScale design also supports exemptions from some traditional requirements, incorporating only those technically relevant to its unique features.

nuscale_ch01_0073.title: Departures
nuscale_ch01_0073.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant’s certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It describes the types of interface requirements (conceptual design information and COL information items), lists the necessary COL information items, and states that COL applicants must provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling water, waste management, and auxiliary systems. It explains that each COL applicant must reference the NuScale FSAR and provide site-specific details, with conceptual design information and security-related content clearly marked and certain details withheld for security reasons. The section also notes that figures illustrate the scope of certified and site-specific designs, as well as the general site layout.
